title: Portfolio project tiering — partitioning the 45-project catalog into core / contextual / exploratory (prior art: spec maturity ladders vs importance tiers)
summary: Prep research for decision #2088 (filed by the 2026-07-01 external consultant review; we:reports/2026-07-02-portfolio-tiering.md). The question: the WE catalog has 45 projects (we:src/_data/projects/*.json) and 98 intents with no explicit must-ship partition, so conformance investment, the adoption narrative, and a newcomer explanation lack a spine — what tier vocabulary, assignment bar, assignment mechanism, and site surfacing should partition it? PRIOR ART: every surveyed ecosystem keeps MATURITY (how far along) and IMPORTANCE/AVAILABILITY (how much it matters) as SEPARATE axes — W3C WD->CR->PR->REC, TC39 stages 0-4, Node.js stability index 0-3, and design-system component lifecycles (USWDS experimental/stable/use-with-caution/deprecated, Polaris alpha/beta/stable/legacy, Carbon experimental/stable) are all MATURITY ladders; the importance axis is a different mechanism: Baseline (web-platform-dx) limited/newly/widely-available is DERIVED from one structured browser-support dataset; Interop's annual focus areas and WICG->standards-track graduation are JUDGMENT-STAMPED against a stated external-demand-evidence bar; npm ecosystems split core-vs-contrib by ownership fact. No system overloads one label set for both axes. CODE GROUND TRUTH: WE already has the maturity axis (status: concept 17 / poc 24 / draft 4 across the 45; public ladder concept->poc->draft->candidate->stable at we:src/project-lifecycle.njk:11-69) and a category axis (standard 37 / utility 7 / ecosystem 1 partitioning the homepage at we:src/index.njk:36-57); cards render status as a we-badge via the SSR weProjectGrid seam (we:.eleventy.js:292-311). No tier-like field exists. The benchmark gap-analysis data (we:src/_data/benchmarkCoverage.json) is capability-keyed, not project-keyed — no domain->project join exists that could COMPUTE a tier, so Baseline-style derivation cannot own the value today; it can only cross-check. Defect found en route (not a #2088 fork): project status is never enum-validated — checkStatus covers Block/Plug etc. but not Project (we:scripts/check-standards.mjs:178-181), LIFECYCLE is concept/draft/experimental/active (we:scripts/check-standards-rules.mjs:726) while projects use poc — three vocabularies for one axis. SYNTHESIS -> forks: Fork 1 (vocabulary) three-tier core/contextual/EXPLORATORY — an evidence-class vocabulary, renaming the stub's 'deferred' which is scheduling language (prioritization vocabulary) and reads as abandonment on a public site — vs the stub's core/contextual/deferred vs two-tier vs overloading the status axis (rejected: importance is orthogonal to maturity; a poc project like webvalidation can be core). Fork 2 (assignment bar) named-consumer evidence: core = a benchmark design system or major framework demonstrably depends on the domain (the Interop/WICG-graduation shape); contextual = unlocks a named constellation consumer (FUI, plateau-app, exercise apps, the WE site); exploratory = no named external consumer yet — vs unstated judgment (the vibe label the review flagged) vs proxy metrics like intent count (measures our own attention, not external demand). Fork 3 (mechanism) an explicitly stamped, enum-validated tier field + required one-line tierEvidence on each we:src/_data/projects/<id>.json — judgment with a stated bar, validated from day one — vs derived-owns-the-value (impossible today, no join) with the benchmark cross-check as a supported advisory gate. Fork 4 (surfacing) in-place: tier badge + tier grouping on the existing landing grids and project detail pages, exploratory grouped last but never hidden — vs segregating exploratory off the main catalog (misrepresents the honest state) vs internal-only (defeats the newcomer-explanation purpose). Supported-by-default: tier and status render side by side (orthogonal axes); tier is re-assignable at review time; tier never gates what a consumer may use. NOT forks: bulk-assigning the 45 projects is follow-on execution of the ratified bar; conformance-investment ordering is prioritization, not a design branch. Vocabulary caution: 'tier' is already overloaded in-repo (backlog Tier A/B/C, capability vision-tiers, open-core pricing tiers) — render the words core/contextual/exploratory, never 'Tier 1/2/3'.
